summary: Pre-built Lumina Default Report Card — backend (7/7 pytest pass) and frontend (all critical flows verified). The default card renders with indigo accent, letter-grade badges (A+/A/A-/B-/C-/etc), 5-dot conduct scale (Punctuality/Participation/Behaviour/Effort), horizontal attendance tile strip (Present/Absent/Late/Excused/% Present), promotion pill (Promoted), teacher+principal comment boxes, parent signature line, and the 'Generated by Lumina-SIS' watermark in the footer. Toolbar (Preview/Export PDF/Print/Send/Lock all) all visible on /report-cards Term Reports tab after generating. Designer toolbar shows new 'Lumina default' Sparkles button which on click writes the audit row and resets canvas_elements=[].

ui_bugs.issue: Route is restricted to allowedRoles=['superuser'] only. The spec says school admins should be able to open the designer directly via /report-template, but they get blocked by ProtectedRoute (admin lands back on dashboard). Admins can still reach the designer via /schools → Edit → Report Designer (embedded mode) because backend POST /report-templates/{sc}/reset-default already accepts admin (verified). Fix: add 'admin' to allowedRoles for /report-template OR clarify the UX so admins always go through /schools.

passed_tests: BACKEND: POST /api/report-templates/SUNF/reset-default as SUNF admin -> 200, design_mode='lumina_default', canvas_elements=[]
BACKEND: POST reset-default as SUNF teacher -> 403
BACKEND: POST reset-default cross-tenant (RVSD admin -> SUNF) -> 403
BACKEND: POST reset-default as JTECH superuser on SUNF -> 200
BACKEND: GET /api/audit-logs?entity_type=report_template returns reset rows for SUNF (action=reset, entity_type=report_template)
BACKEND: PUT /api/report-templates/SUNF with design_mode='canvas' + canvas_elements persists; subsequent GET returns design_mode='canvas' with the elements; cleanup reset back to default
FE: SUNF admin /report-cards Term Reports tab generates 11 report cards; 'Generated by Lumina-SIS' watermark count = 11 (one per card)
FE: Letter-grade colored badges visible (A, A-, B, B-, C, C-) in Grade column
FE: Horizontal attendance tile strip (Present/Absent/Late/Excused/% Present) visible
FE: Toolbar after Generate shows preview-report-btn, export-pdf-btn, print-report-btn, send-to-parents-btn, lock-report-cards-btn
FE: /report-template?school=SUNF (as JTECH superuser) loads designer, shows reset-default-btn with text 'Lumina default' and Sparkles icon

FE: Clicking reset-default-btn shows confirm dialog (accepted programmatically) and toast 'Reverted to Lumina default. The new template will appear on the next report card generation.'; canvas cleared (empty grid)

FE: Filename builder code path produces [LastName]_[FirstName]_ReportCard_[Term]_[AcademicYear].pdf when activeTab==='term-reports' && reportCards.length===1 (verified by code inspection at ReportsPage.js:950-955)
action_items: App.js: add 'admin' to allowedRoles for /report-template so school admins can open the designer directly per spec (or document the /schools → Edit path as the only entry point for admins).

Single-student PDF export filename pattern is implemented but was NOT exercised end-to-end via a real download in this run (jsPDF triggers a download which is non-trivial to capture in headless Playwright). Code inspection confirms the [LastName]_[FirstName]_ReportCard_[Term]_[AcademicYear].pdf pattern in ReportsPage.js:949-955. Main agent can self-verify by clicking Export PDF on a single-student term report.
critical_code_review_comments: ReportsPage.js is now 1405 lines and ReportTemplateDesigner.js is 1299 lines — both well above the 700-line guideline. Strongly recommend splitting (extract PdfExporter, ReportCardRenderer, PreviewModal hooks into separate files for ReportsPage; extract Canvas, Toolbar, Properties panel into separate files for the designer).
server.py reset-default endpoint requires the SCHOOL admin (current_user.school_code == sc) — verified working. Consider including the audit-row id in the response for downstream UI linking.

build_default_template returns is_locked_default=True but no endpoint actually enforces 'locked' semantics on subsequent PUTs — currently PUT will happily overwrite the default. If the lock is meant to be authoritative, either reject PUT when is_locked_default && requester is admin (not superuser), or rename the flag to is_default_template to remove the misleading semantics.

The Lumina default card in screenshot renders cleanly within the legal canvas. No second-page overflow observed for a sample student (Sofia Davis, 11 subjects, ~95% present). However, very long teacher/principal comments could push content; consider clamp/scroll on those <textarea> areas in print.

success_rate.frontend: ~95% (one route-permission gap; PDF download filename verified via code inspection only)

test_credentials: SUNF admin/Admin@123, SUNF teacher sarah.thompson.sunf/Teacher@123, RVSD admin/Admin@123, JTECH jtech.innovations@outlook.com/Xekleidoma@1. SUNF template was reset back to lumina_default at the end of testing.
seed_data_creation: Multiple reset-default writes to audit_logs for SUNF (action=reset, entity_type=report_template). No student/class data created.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Designer URL must include ?school=<CODE> query param (e.g. /report-template?school=SUNF) otherwise the page shows 'No school specified.' /report-template route currently restricts to superuser in App.js (line 180); admins are bounced to /dashboard. Backend reset-default endpoint correctly accepts admin role. Backend BASE_URL helper in /app/backend/tests/test_lumina_default.py falls back to reading /app/frontend/.env if REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL not in os.environ.
